---
title: "Path to Encryption"
subtitle: "The mathematics that lets you keep secrets in a hostile world"
---

## What you can do at the end of this path

- Understand why every modern cryptographic system rests on the difficulty of
  certain number-theoretic problems.
- Implement RSA from scratch and explain why it is secure.
- Read modern crypto papers on elliptic curves and lattice-based schemes
  without the maths blocking you.

## The path

Encryption is, surprisingly, the most number-theoretic of the applied
domains. The path runs through arithmetic, then abstract algebra, then
algebraic number theory.

```{mermaid}
flowchart LR
    A[Tier 1<br/>Divisibility & Primes] --> B[Tier 2<br/>Modular arithmetic intro]
    B --> C[Tier 3<br/>Combinatorics, exponents, logs]
    C --> D[Tier 5<br/>Discrete math + intro number theory]
    D --> E[Tier 6<br/>Abstract algebra: groups, rings, fields]
    E --> F[Tier 7 — Encryption track:<br/>Algebraic number theory<br/>Elliptic curves<br/>Lattice-based crypto]

    classDef built fill:#c8e6c9,stroke:#2e7d32
    class A built
```

## Where to start right now

Even at the very first lesson — [*What is a
Number?*](../lessons/tier-1-foundations/01-what-is-a-number/index.qmd) — the
*Connect* beat already names how the existence of prime numbers becomes the
hinge of modern cryptography. Read it through and follow forward to
**Divisibility and Primes**.

## What's built / what's planned

- ✅ Tier 1: *What is a Number?* (exemplar lesson with encryption *Connect*)
- 📋 Tier 1: Divisibility & Primes (the next required step)
- 📋 Everything beyond Tier 1 is planned but not yet authored.
